EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of two Pacific storms tracked across the region bringing winter conditions to the mountains of eastern Utah. The first system dragged a cold front across the state and spread snowfall across the eastern Utah mountains, as well as some of the lower valleys. The second low pressure system dropped down into the Great Basin region and formed a closed low pressure system near the four corners area. This continued mountain snowfall across the east central and southeast Utah mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall of 5 to 12 inches occurred across most of the area with locally higher amounts above the 9,000 foot level, including 16 inches at the La Sal Mountain SNOTEL site. Wind gusts up to 30 mph produced blowing and drifting snow.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall of 5 to 12 inches occurred above the 8,000 foot level, with 2 to 5 inches at lower elevations. Wind gusts up to 35 mph produced blowing and drifting snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A very strong, moist, and unseasonably cold winter storm struck the central intermountain region. Heavy snowfall occurred in most areas across eastern Utah and western Colorado. EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms produced heavy rainfall that resulted in flash flooding across Hastings Road, a county road in Grand County that parallels the Green River.  A large amount of mud and debris covered the road and trapped about twenty campers on the north end of the road until heavy equipment cleared the road late the next day.  A man riding a horse was sent into the river when the bank washed out underneath the horse.  The man was able to swim out of the river, though the horse did not survive.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall of 5 to 8 inches occurred above 8,000 feet. Wind gusts up to 30 to 40 mph also occurred.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong and cold Pacific storm system pushed a cold front southeastward across eastern Utah, resulting in heavy snowfall and gusty winds across the easteran Utah mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall of 5 to 12 inches occurred above the 7,500 foot level. Strong winds with gusts up to 30 to 50 mph also occurred.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front moved across eastern Utah bringing periods of heavy snowfall and strong winds to the eastern Utah mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall amounts around 5 inches occurred across the area with winds that gusted up to 20 to 30 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front moved across eastern Utah bringing periods of heavy snowfall and strong winds to the eastern Utah mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall of 5 to 6 inches occurred above the 8,000 foot level.  Strong winds gusted up to 25 to 50 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A severe thunderstorm produced very strong outflow winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorm winds blew down some old trees and broke off large branches from other trees throughout the town of Bluff.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold and stable airmass produced areas of dense fog in the Eastern Uinta Basin throughout the evening hours of New Year's Eve.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Dense fog formed near the town of Vernal and extended eastward toward Jensen. Visibilities were frequently reduced to near one quarter mile. This event continued through January 1, 2010. Please see the January 2010 Storm Data Publication for the continuation of this event.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold and stable airmass resulted in dense fog formation across the lower valleys of western Colorado and eastern Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Dense fog frequently reduced visibilities to below one quarter mile, particularly along highway 40 between the Colorado border and Ballard, Utah.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A Pacific trough and a series of upper level disturbances brought a prolonged period of snow and strong winds to the mountains of eastern Utah. Additionally, cold air trapped in the valleys resulted in areas of freezing rain.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall of 12 to 15 inches occurred above 8000 feet while wind gusts of 20 to 35 mph produced blowing and drifting snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An early spring season storm dropped out of the Pacific Northwest and into the Great Basin. A cold front passed from north to south across the region and brought heavy snowfall and strong gusty winds to the eastern Utah mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall of 5 to 8 inches occurred with wind gusts of up to 25 to 35 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A Pacific trough and a series of upper level disturbances brought a prolonged period of snow and strong winds to the mountains of eastern Utah. Additionally, cold air trapped in the valleys resulted in areas of freezing rain.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall of 6 to 12 inches occurred over the eastern half of the area. The highest amounts occurred around Jensen. Wind gusts of 15 to 25 mph produced some blowing and drifting of snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A Pacific trough and a series of upper level disturbances brought a prolonged period of snow and strong winds to the mountains of eastern Utah. Additionally, cold air trapped in the valleys resulted in areas of freezing rain.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Freezing rain caused icy roads and resulted in numerous vehicle accidents.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A moist and stable airmass on top of snow cover resulted in dense fog across the Eastern Uinta Basin.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Dense fog reduced visibility to 1/4 mile or less in places.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A moist Pacific storm system moved inland over the Pacific coast and then tracked through the Great Basin region, spreading snowfall across much of eastern Utah and Western Colorado. The best upper level support associated with this storm was positioned over southern Colorado and southeast Utah, which received the heaviest snowfall.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall of 3 to 5 inches occurred across the area creating icy roads and hazardous travel conditions, especially along I-70 where a fatal car accident occurred. A Moab resident was driving west on I-70 near Green River, Utah around 12:16 pm when he lost control of his vehicle and slid off the interstate near mile marker 191. The vehicle flipped over and landed on its roof, leaving the victim trapped inside, only to be rescued by the Moab Fire Department. He was rushed to Allen Memorial Hospital where, according to Utah Highway Patrol reports, he died due to accident related injuries.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another fall-like storm moved into Utah in mid August. Showers and thunderstorms with this system caused strong gusty winds across the state.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Wasatch Hills sensor recorded a maximum wind gust of 60 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain fell in the Manila area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms produced severe weather events across northwest Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Golfball size hail struck the Naples area.  Gardens and crops were decimated.  Cars were dented and windows broken.  Many houses and businesses incurred damage to siding and roofs, as well as broken windows.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms produced severe weather events across northwest Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Fast moving hailstones up to the size of ping pong balls broke windows, dented vehicles, and damaged siding on residences.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms produced severe weather events across northwest Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Golfball sized hailstones dented vehicles and broke windshields and headlights traveling along highway 191 in the eastern Uinta Mountains.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms produced severe weather events across northwest Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A tornado was observed by a number of people in the Pelican Lake area.  The tornado was first seen as it came up over a hill just west of Pelican Lake.  The tornado then traveled across Pelican Lake and turned white as it pick up water off the lake.  On the east shore of the lake, the tornado tore up some trees and flipped over a trailer used for living quarters at a man camp.  Nobody was in the trailer at the time of the event.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong and moist Pacific storm system moved into the Rocky Mountain Region spreading heavy snowfall across the western Colorado and eastern Utah mountains, as well as many of the higher valleys of northern Colorado and Utah. Warm air associated with this system brought a mix of rain and snow to most of the central and southern valleys, with primarily rain falling over southeast Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall amounts across the eastern Uinta Mountains ranged from 5 to 12 inches. Wind gusts up to 35 mph were reported, causing blowing and drifting snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong and moist Pacific storm system moved into the Rocky Mountain Region spreading heavy snowfall across the western Colorado and eastern Utah mountains, as well as many of the higher valleys of northern Colorado and Utah. Warm air associated with this system brought a mix of rain and snow to most of the central and southern valleys, with primarily rain falling over southeast Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall amounts across the southeast Utah mountains ranged from 5 to 12 inches with locally higher amounts reported in the Abajo mountains, including 15 inches at the Camp Jackson SNOTEL site. Wind gusts up to 55 mph generated areas of blowing and drifting snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong and moist Pacific storm system moved into the Rocky Mountain Region spreading heavy snowfall across the western Colorado and eastern Utah mountains, as well as many of the higher valleys of northern Colorado and Utah. Warm air associated with this system brought a mix of rain and snow to most of the central and southern valleys, with primarily rain falling over southeast Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall amounts across the area ranged from 12 to 15 inches with wind gusts of 45 mph generating blowing and drifting snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong and moist Pacific storm system moved into the Rocky Mountain Region spreading heavy snowfall across the western Colorado and eastern Utah mountains, as well as many of the higher valleys of northern Colorado and Utah. Warm air associated with this system brought a mix of rain and snow to most of the central and southern valleys, with primarily rain falling over southeast Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Most of the snow across the area fell within a 10 hour period beginning late on the 12th and lasting through mid morning on the 13th. Snowfall amounts generally ranged from 5 to 9 inches. However, locally higher amounts were reported just north of Vernal, including 11 inches 1 mile north of town and 13 inches 3 miles northeast of town.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of moist Pacific storm systems moved through Utah over a 3-day period. The storm brought wet, heavy snow to much of the state, with the heaviest snow across both the valleys and mountains of northern Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: SunCrest received 14 inches of new snow and Alpine received 9.5 new inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of moist Pacific storm systems moved through Utah over a 3-day period. The storm brought wet, heavy snow to much of the state, with the heaviest snow across both the valleys and mountains of northern Utah.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Recorded wind gusts included 60 mph at the Cedar City ASOS, 61 mph at the Enterprise RAWS, 58 mph at the Milford ASOS, and 62 mph at the I-15 at Black Ridge sensor.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The last in a series of quick-hitting winter storms was the strongest, dropping significant snowfall on the mountains of the entire state of Utah, as well as the northern Wasatch Front. Significant snowfall began on February 8th for the southern mountains of Utah, and on February 9th for most locations in northern Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Kolob SNOTEL received 3.60 inches liquid equivalent precipitation and Midway Valley SNOTEL received 2.60 inches liquid equivalent, or about 30-40 inches of new snow over a 2 day period for the southern mountains.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front entered Utah early on March 9th and then stalled, bringing a period of heavy snowfall to the far northern portions of the state. The front then continued to slowly move south, bringing heavy snow to the rest of the Wasatch Front and parts of central Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Ben Lomond Peak SNOTEL received 1.30 inches of liquid equivalent precipitation for the storm. Based on snow ratios at similar elevations, this was approximately 20 inches of new snow in less than 24 hours.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy snow was observed in both Big and Little Cottonwood Canyons, with Brighton Crest receiving 27 new inches of snow for the storm, while Solitude and Alta both received 23 new inches of snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A deepening storm system moving across the Great Basin brought strong, gusty pre-frontal winds. Winds were strongest in the west deserts of Utah, as well as along the Wasatch Range.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Salt Lake and Tooele valleys experienced scattered severe wind gusts through this period, including gusts of 72 mph and 62 mph at Vernon Hill, and 59 mph at the Great Salt Lake Marina. A few large trees were also knocked down in Salt Lake county.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A very moist Pacific storm system dropped heavy snow on northern Utah, with the Ogden Valley receiving higher amounts than even the surrounding mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall totals along the northern Wasatch Front included 18 inches near Weber State University, 17 inches at Layton Bench, 14 inches in Roy and South Ogden, and 12 inches at Northeast Ogden Bench.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A small but heavy band of snow associated with the cold front moved through Oak City over a 3 hour period, dropping 6 inches of new snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A deepening storm system moving across the Great Basin brought strong, gusty pre-frontal winds. Winds were strongest in the west deserts of Utah, as well as along the Wasatch Range.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Scattered severe wind gust reports occurred over the period of a couple days, including 88 mph at Ogden Peak and 82 mph at Snowbasin-Straw Top.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front entered Utah early on March 9th and then stalled, bringing a period of heavy snowfall to the far northern portions of the state. The front then continued to slowly move south, bringing heavy snow to the rest of the Wasatch Front and parts of central Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Cache Valley received the most snowfall because of the stalled front, with reports of 25 new inches of snow at Paradise, 20 new inches at Logan USU and Hyrum, and 14.5 new inches of snow for the storm at River Heights.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong and moist Pacific storm system brought heavy snow to northern and central Utah, with particularly heavy snowfall across the higher terrain.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowbasin Mid-Bowl received 22 inches of new snow with 2.61 liquid equivalent, while Emigration Canyon received 15 inches of snow. Other liquid measurements across the area included 4.20 inches at Farmington Upper SNOTEL and 3.40 inches at Ben Lomond Peak, indicating that as much as 30 to 40 inches of snow likely fell across the higher terrain.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong and moist Pacific storm system brought heavy snow to northern and central Utah, with particularly heavy snowfall across the higher terrain.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Liberty received 16 inches of new snow for the event. Other storm totals across the Wasatch mountain valleys included 14 inches at Morgan, 12 inches at Soapstone Guard Station, and 11 inches of snow in Eden.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A deepening storm system moving across the Great Basin brought strong, gusty pre-frontal winds. Winds were strongest in the west deserts of Utah, as well as along the Wasatch Range.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Maximum measured wind gusts included 77 mph at Cardiff Peak and 81 mph at Sundance Arrowhead. In addition, the winds also derailed cables from a support tower pulley system on the Peruvian lift at Snowbird, prompting the evacuation of 89 patrons as the lift automatically shut down.

CZ_NAME: GREAT SALT LAKE DESERT AND MOUNTAINS
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong winter storm produced heavy snowfall over the Wasatch Range, while the associated cold front brought strong winds to much of the state of Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several very strong wind gusts were recorded across the west deserts of Utah, including a wind gust of 73 mph at the Callao Gate sensor. Other gusts included 69 mph at the Causeway sensor, 67 mph at Wendover Point of Entry, and 65 mph at the North Salt Flats sensor. In addition, three semitrailers were blown over by the strong winds along I-80. Some small trees were also blown over near Wendover.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong winter storm produced heavy snowfall over the Wasatch Range, while the associated cold front brought strong winds to much of the state of Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A sensor in Grantsville recorded a maximum wind gust of 62 mph. Also, extensive roof damage to several homes in the Draper area was reported.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong and moist Pacific storm system brought heavy snow to northern and central Utah, with particularly heavy snowfall across the higher terrain.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Pine Creek SNOTEL received 1.70 inches liquid equivalent precipitation, or approximately 21 inches of new snow based on snow ratios at similar elevations.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: One last winter storm arrived in Utah on the last day of March, bringing heavy snow and gusty winds to the Wasatch Mountains. Note that this episode continued into April.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Tony Grove SNOTEL received 1.60 inches liquid equivalent precipitation, or approximately 18 inches new snow for the storm. In addition, the Ogden Peak sensor recorded a maximum wind gust of 83 mph. Note that this event continued into April.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front entered Utah early on March 9th and then stalled, bringing a period of heavy snowfall to the far northern portions of the state. The front then continued to slowly move south, bringing heavy snow to the rest of the Wasatch Front and parts of central Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: West Jordan received 6 inches of new snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front entered Utah early on March 9th and then stalled, bringing a period of heavy snowfall to the far northern portions of the state. The front then continued to slowly move south, bringing heavy snow to the rest of the Wasatch Front and parts of central Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Springville received 7 inches of new snow for the event.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A deepening storm system moving across the Great Basin brought strong, gusty pre-frontal winds. Winds were strongest in the west deserts of Utah, as well as along the Wasatch Range.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Milford Airport ASOS measured a wind gust of 58 mph.

CZ_NAME: GREAT SALT LAKE DESERT AND MOUNTAINS
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A Pacific storm system created gusty winds across the state. Snowfall was widespread across northern Utah, but the heaviest snow was restricted to the higher terrain.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Multiple sensors in western Utah recorded strong wind gusts, with the strongest being a 65 mph gust at South Mountain. Other gusts included 64 mph at Camel Back Mountain and 60 mph at Lakeside Mountain.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A Pacific storm system created gusty winds across the state. Snowfall was widespread across northern Utah, but the heaviest snow was restricted to the higher terrain.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Bullfrog Marina sensor recorded a wind gust of 65 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A Pacific storm system created gusty winds across the state. Snowfall was widespread across northern Utah, but the heaviest snow was restricted to the higher terrain.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Alta received a storm total of 22 inches of new snow. Winds were also gusty in the area, with a peak gust of 71 mph at Solitude.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong and moist Pacific storm system brought heavy snow to northern and central Utah, with particularly heavy snowfall across the higher terrain.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Brighton Crest received 41 inches of new snow for the event, while Alta received 40 inches and Snowbird received 31 inches of snow. Winds were also gusty along the peaks, with a maximum recorded wind gust of 78 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong and moist Pacific storm system brought heavy snow to northern and central Utah, with particularly heavy snowfall across the higher terrain.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Hayden Fork SNOTEL received 1.90 inches of liquid equivalent precipitation for the storm, or approximately 25 inches of new snow based on snow ratios at similar elevations.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Gusty pre-frontal winds were observed near the Salt Lake and Tooele valleys ahead of an incoming winter storm.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Widely scattered gusts greater than 58 mph were observed near the Salt Lake and Tooele valleys, including a 60 mph gust at the Great Salt Lake Marina and a 61 mph gust at the South Mountain sensor.

CZ_NAME: GREAT SALT LAKE DESERT AND MOUNTAINS
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Virga and rain showers across the Great Salt Lake Desert caused a period of gusty winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Virga and showers with no lightning production created strong gusty winds across the Great Salt Lake Desert, including gusts of 74 mph at West Salt Flats, a 61 mph gust at Simpson Springs, and a 59 mph wind gust recorded at the I-80 at Grassey sensor.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter storm arrived in Utah on the last day of March and continued into April, bringing heavy snow and gusty winds to the Wasatch Mountains. The associated cold front also brought gusty winds to the west deserts of Utah. Note that this episode began in March.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Tony Grove SNOTEL received 1.60 inches liquid equivalent precipitation, or approximately 18 inches of new snow for the storm. In addition, the Ogden Peak sensor recorded a maximum wind gust of 83 mph. Note that this event began in March.

CZ_NAME: GREAT SALT LAKE DESERT AND MOUNTAINS
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter storm arrived in Utah on the last day of March and continued into April, bringing heavy snow and gusty winds to the Wasatch Mountains. The associated cold front also brought gusty winds to the west deserts of Utah. Note that this episode began in March.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Multiple strong wind gusts were recorded across the Great Salt Lake and Great Salt Lake Desert, including a 73 mph gust at the Rosebud RAWS and a 67 mph gust at Promontory Point.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter storm arrived in Utah on the last day of March and continued into April, bringing heavy snow and gusty winds to the Wasatch Mountains. The associated cold front also brought gusty winds to the west deserts of Utah. Note that this episode began in March.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Alta received 19 inches of new snow, while Snowbird received 17 inches. Winds were also gusty, with a maximum wind gust of 103 mph recorded along the peaks. Note that this event began in March.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An early spring season storm dropped southeastward out of the Pacific northwest and into the Great Basin. The storm pushed a cold front from north to south across eastern Utah, which resulted in heavy snow and strong gusty winds over the mountains of eastern Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This event continued through April 2. Snowfall amounts ranging from 5 to 8 inches occurred across the area along with wind gusts of up to 25 to 40 mph. For a continuation of this event please see the April 2009 Storm Data publication.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An early spring season storm dropped southeastward out of the Pacific northwest and into the Great Basin. The storm pushed a cold front from north to south across eastern Utah, which resulted in heavy snow and strong gusty winds over the mountains of eastern Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This event continued through April 2. Snowfall of 5 to 10 inches occurred over the area with strong winds that gusted up to 30 to 45 mph. For a continuation of this event please see the April 2009 Storm Data publication.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong low pressure storm system over the Baja Peninsula spread deep moisture across the Great Basin. Southerly flow began snowfall over the southern Utah mountains, which then spread across the central and northern Utah mountains as the system tracked over eastern Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall of 5 to 12 inches occurred while wind gusts of 20 to 30 mph caused blowing and drifting snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong low pressure storm system over the Baja Peninsula spread deep moisture across the Great Basin. Southerly flow began snowfall over the southern Utah mountains, which then spread across the central and northern Utah mountains as the system tracked over eastern Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall of 5 to 12 inches occurred while strong wind gusts of 30 to 50 mph caused blowing and drifting snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong low pressure storm system over the Baja Peninsula spread deep moisture across the Great Basin. Southerly flow began snowfall over the southern Utah mountains, which then spread across the central and northern Utah mountains as the system tracked over eastern Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall of 5 to 11 inches occurred above the 7,500 foot level. Wind gusts of 20 to 30 mph caused blowing and drifting snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An early spring season storm dropped out of the Pacific Northwest and into the Great Basin. A cold front passed from north to south across the region and brought heavy snowfall and strong gusty winds to the eastern Utah mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall of 5 to 8 inches occurred across the area with wind gusts of 25 to 40 mph.  See the March 2009 Storm Data Publication for the beginning of this event.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An early spring season storm dropped out of the Pacific Northwest and into the Great Basin. A cold front passed from north to south across the region and brought heavy snowfall and strong gusty winds to the eastern Utah mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall of 5 to 10 inches occurred with strong winds that gusted up to 30 to 45 mph.  See the March 2009 Storm Data Publication for the beginning of this event.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong Pacific winter storm moved across the region spreading significant amounts of snowfall over the mountains of eastern Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall of 5 to 7 inches occurred along with wind gusts of 20 to 30 mph that caused blowing and drifting snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A Pacific storm system moved east across Arizona and near the Four Corners and brought a surge of moisture to eastern Utah. Behind this system, another storm quickly moved over the region from the northwest and brought colder air and more snow to the area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: One to two feet of snow fell across the area above the 8000 foot level with lesser amounts at lower elevations. Wind gusts of 25 to 50 mph produced blowing and drifting snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A Pacific storm system moved east across Arizona and near the Four Corners and brought a surge of moisture to eastern Utah. Behind this system, another storm quickly moved over the region from the northwest and brought colder air and more snow to the area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Widespread snowfall of 3 to 6 inches occurred with locally higher  amounts, including 8 inches in the Blanding area. Wind gusts of 20 to 30 mph produced blowing and drifting snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A low pressure system preceded by a number of weak disturbances brought snow and gusty winds to the mountains of east central and northeast Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall of 5 to 7 inches occurred while wind gusts of 25 to 40 mph produced blowing and drifting snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A low pressure system preceded by a number of weak disturbances brought snow and gusty winds to the mountains of east central and northeast Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall of 5 to 11 inches occurred while wind gusts of 20 to 30 mph produced blowing and drifting snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Moist and stable conditions resulted in dense fog across the area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Dense fog reduced visibilities to less than 1/4 mile from Vernal to the Utah border with Colorado.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A Pacific spring storm system dropped into the Great Basin and across the forecast area. A cold front accompanying this system brought widespread snowfall across the mountains and some of the valleys of eastern Utah. Strong southwest winds ahead of the front also caused blowing and drifting snow.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy snowfall of 12 to 24 inches occurred. Winds also gusted 25 to 40 mph and caused blowing and drifting of snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A Pacific spring storm system dropped into the Great Basin and across the forecast area. A cold front accompanying this system brought widespread snowfall across the mountains and some of the valleys of eastern Utah. Strong southwest winds ahead of the front also caused blowing and drifting snow.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy snowfall of 10 to 15 inches occurred above the 8,000 foot level. Wind gusts of 25 to 40 mph caused blowing and drifting snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A Pacific spring storm system dropped into the Great Basin and across the forecast area. A cold front accompanying this system brought widespread snowfall across the mountains and some of the valleys of eastern Utah. Strong southwest winds ahead of the front also caused blowing and drifting snow.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy snowfall of 12 to 23 inches occurred above the 8,000 foot level. Widespread wind gusts of 30 to 50 mph, with local gusts of up to 64 mph, caused blowing and drifting snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A Pacific spring storm system dropped into the Great Basin and across the forecast area. A cold front accompanying this system brought widespread snowfall across the mountains and some of the valleys of eastern Utah. Strong southwest winds ahead of the front also caused blowing and drifting snow.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall of 2 to 4 inches occurred over the area with wind gusts of 20 to 30 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Widespread freezing temperatures early in the growing season threatened vegetation.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Minimum temperatures ranged from 23 to 26 degrees Fahrenheit.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Very cold air poured into eastern Utah and allowed overnight temperatures to drop below freezing across the lower Colorado River basin.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Minimum temperatures ranged from 16 to 25 degrees Fahrenheit.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong Pacific storm system and cold front moved across the area bringing significant snowfall to the mountains of northeast Utah. Strong southwest winds ahead of the front caused blowing and drifting snow.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall of 5 to 12 inches fell across the area. Wind gusts up to 40 mph caused blowing and drifting snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A severe thunderstorm produced very strong outflow winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An intense thunderstorm produced strong outflow winds in the Valley of the Gods area.  Unsecured items at a residence were blown over.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a water treatment plant in Daggett County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck the Red Canyon water treatment plant and destroyed the USFS' transducer.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A Pacific storm system and associated cold front produced significant snowfall across the Eastern Uinta Mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall of 5 to 9 inches occurred above 7500 feet.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Severe thunderstorms created gusty winds and large hail in northern Utah. In addition, southern Utah experienced isolated severe gusts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Bullfrog Marina sensor recorded a wind gust of 68 mph.

CZ_NAME: UTAHS DIXIE AND ZION NATIONAL PARK
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Severe thunderstorms created gusty winds and large hail in northern Utah. In addition, southern Utah experienced isolated severe gusts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Virga created strong winds in far southwest Utah, with the St. George AWOS recording a wind gust of 59 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Severe thunderstorms created gusty winds and large hail in northern Utah. In addition, southern Utah experienced isolated severe gusts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Law enforcement in Cache County observed hail near Newton that they estimated to be the size of golf balls, or 1.75 inches in diameter.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Large hail was observed in northern Utah on both June 8th and 9th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Hailstones with a 0.75 inch diameter were observed in Providence.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Large hail was observed in northern Utah on both June 8th and 9th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Local television media reported 0.75 inch diameter hail in Cottonwood Heights.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Severe thunderstorms created gusty winds and large hail in northern Utah. In addition, southern Utah experienced isolated severe gusts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A line of thunderstorms produced severe gusty winds across the Dugway Proving Ground Mesonet, with the strongest recorded gust being 79 mph at Camel Back Mountain. Other recorded gusts included 63 mph at White Sage, 61 mph at Salt Flats, 61 mph at Tower Grid, and 60 mph at the I-80 at Grassey sensor.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Severe hail and winds were reported across Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Brimstone Reservoir RAWS recorded a wind gust of 63 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Severe hail and winds were reported across Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: One inch hail was reported in Sunnyside.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Severe hail and winds were reported across Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: One inch hail was reported near West Haven.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A brief period of heavy rain caused a flash flood at Capitol Reef National Park.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A visitor to Capitol Reef National Park reported a flash flood in the Cathedral Valley area of the park after a brief period of heavy rain.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong to severe thunderstorms were observed in northwest Utah on the first two days of June, with both large hail and strong winds reported.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained spotter reported 0.75 inch hail near Tremonton.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong to severe thunderstorms were observed in northwest Utah on the first two days of June, with both large hail and strong winds reported.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Multiple severe wind gusts were recorded with this thunderstorm, the strongest of which was a 69 mph gust at the Salt Flats sensor.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Large hail fell in portions of Uintah County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Hail up to golf ball size pummeled the Randlett area, damaging crops and gardens.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Large hail fell in portions of Uintah County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Golf ball size hail struck an area of US Highway 40 southeast of Jensen.  Some vehicles and camper trailers sustained damage.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Flash flooding occurred in the town of Liberty due to heavy rain.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flash flooding in Liberty included a road that was washed out, as well as minor landscape damage to a few homes.

CZ_NAME: GREAT SALT LAKE DESERT AND MOUNTAINS
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms moved across northern Utah on the afternoon of May 3rd, some of them severe. Both gusty winds and severe hail were observed, and an EF0 tornado touched down in Willard, Utah. In addition, strong gusty winds ocurred near the Great Salt Lake after the cold frontal passage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several strong post-frontal wind gusts were recorded across the Great Salt Lake, including 71 mph at Promontory Point, 66 mph at Gunnison Island, and 62 mph at Hat Island.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A tornado was spotted and photographed by multiple observers in Beaver County, Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A couple different observers saw a tornado touch down 5 miles west of Milford, and one took pictures of the tornado. The tornado was in an uninhabited area and did no damage.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms moved across northern Utah on the afternoon of May 3rd, some of them severe. Both gusty winds and severe hail were observed, and an EF0 tornado touched down in Willard, Utah. In addition, strong gusty winds ocurred near the Great Salt Lake after the cold frontal passage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Hail with a 0.75 inch diameter was observed in Perry, Utah. The spotter also reported that the hail accumulated 2 inches deep.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms moved across northern Utah on the afternoon of May 3rd, some of them severe. Both gusty winds and severe hail were observed, and an EF0 tornado touched down in Willard, Utah. In addition, strong gusty winds ocurred near the Great Salt Lake after the cold frontal passage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A magnitude EF0 tornado touched down in Willard. The tornado path was one mile, starting at 75 East 300 South and ending at 300 East 230 South. The damage from the tornado included damaged siding, several uprooted trees, a couple of snapped trees, and a tossed empty snowmobile trailer.

CZ_NAME: GREAT SALT LAKE DESERT AND MOUNTAINS
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Virga in the deserts of northwest Utah created strong gusty winds over a wide area throughout the afternoon of May 19th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Virga, which was not producing any lightning, created gusty winds across the West Desert. Recorded wind gusts included 69 mph at Lower Cedar Mountain, 69 mph at Camel Back Mountain, 68 mph at Upper Cedar Mountain, and 65 mph at Callao Gate.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced a severe wind gust at Milford, Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Milford ASOS recorded a 62 mph wind gust.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms moved across northern Utah on the afternoon of May 3rd, some of them severe. Both gusty winds and severe hail were observed, and an EF0 tornado touched down in Willard, Utah. In addition, strong gusty winds ocurred near the Great Salt Lake after the cold frontal passage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 62 mph wind gust was recorded at the I-80 UDOT sensor at mile post 78.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong gusty winds were observed in northern Utah on July 18th and 19th, both due to thunderstorms and virga.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 60 mph wind gust was recorded at the I-84 at White's Valley sensor.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong gusty winds were observed in northern Utah on July 18th and 19th, both due to thunderstorms and virga.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Vernon Hill sensor recorded a maximum wind gust of 76 mph.

CZ_NAME: GREAT SALT LAKE DESERT AND MOUNTAINS
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Virga created severe wind gusts in the northwest deserts of Utah on July 4th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The North Salt Flats sensor recorded a maximum wind gust of 61 mph. Also, the Camel Back Mountain sensor recorded a wind gust of 58 mph.

CZ_NAME: GREAT SALT LAKE DESERT AND MOUNTAINS
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong gusty winds were observed in northern Utah on July 18th and 19th, both due to thunderstorms and virga.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Camel Back Mountain sensor in the Dugway mesonet recorded a maximum wind gust of 75 mph associated with virga.

CZ_NAME: GREAT SALT LAKE DESERT AND MOUNTAINS
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An unseasonably cold and potent storm system moved into Utah beginning on August 6th. There were strong winds with this storm, both ahead of and behind the associated cold front.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Wind gusts across the Great Salt Lake Desert and Mountains included 69 mph at Hat Island, 65 mph at the West Salt Flats sensor, and 58 mph at the North Salt Flats sensor. These winds also blew over a horse trailer along I-80 in Tooele County.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Overnight low temperatures dropped below freezing across the Eastern Uinta Basin of northeast Utah for the first time of the season, after a cold front moved across the region and ushered in much colder air. This esentially ended the growing season for this area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Minimum temperature across the area dropped to between 27 and 30 degrees.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms produced severe weather events across northwest Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A tornado tracked through a small section of Vernal, mostly staying between houses.  A number of trees had large limbs broken off and some minor damage occurred to sheds.  The tornado was seen by many area residents and photographed by at least three persons.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A moist westerly flow with embedded weather disturbances brought periods of snow to northern Utah over two days. The snow became heavy at times, especially over the Cottonwood Canyons and Ogden Valley.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Alta received a storm total of 37 inches new snow. Snowbird received 24 inches, and Brighton Crest received 22 inches of snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck an oil battery.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck an oil battery, heavily damaging two large oil tanks and other equipment.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front entered Utah early on March 9th and then stalled, bringing a period of heavy snowfall to the far northern portions of the state. The front then continued to slowly move south, bringing heavy snow to the rest of the Wasatch Front and parts of central Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Brigham City received 13 new inches of snow for the storm, while Northeast Ogden Bench received 11.5 inches of snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A moist winter storm brought heavy snow to the higher terrain of Utah, with most of the precipitation in the valleys falling as moderate to heavy rain.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Chalk Creek #1 SNOTEL received 2.75 inches liquid equivalent, or approximately 35 inches of new snow for the event.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A moist winter storm brought heavy snow to the higher terrain of Utah, with most of the precipitation in the valleys falling as moderate to heavy rain.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Clear Creek #1 SNOTEL received a storm total of 2.30 inches liquid equivalent precipitation, or approximately 30 inches of new snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A moist winter storm brought heavy snow to the higher terrain of Utah, with most of the precipitation in the valleys falling as moderate to heavy rain.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Pine Creek SNOTEL received 2.30 inches liquid equivalent precipitation, or approximately 30 inches of new snow for the event.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms in the northern half of Utah produced severe wind gusts over the first two days of July.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Vernon Hill sensor recorded a wind gust of 63 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms in southeast Utah produced heavy rainfall that resulted in flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms produced heavy rainfall that brought a wall of water 5 to 7 feet deep down Kane Creek which inundated a low water crossing of Kane Creek Road and washed away three newer model SUVs.  All three vehicles were destroyed.  All 17 tourists, which included 9 children, abandoned their vehicles just seconds prior to the flash flood arrival.  Eventually, the entire valley filled up with water.  The situation was recorded on videotape by one of the survivors.  Several sheriffs deputies who were enroute to rescue the tourists became stranded near Hoorah Pass by a subsequent mudslide.  Two helicopters from Salt Lake City arrived to the area later and were used to rescue the tourists and sheriffs deputies.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms in the northern half of Utah produced severe wind gusts over the first two days of July.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The US-40 at Starvation sensor recorded a wind gust of 59 mph. In addition, trees were knocked down in Tabiona near State Route 35.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Gusty winds were observed in the northern and western parts of Utah, both due to thunderstorms and virga.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Clifton Flat sensor recorded a wind gust of 58 mph, followed by a 60 mph wind gust at the North Salt Flats sensor.

CZ_NAME: GREAT SALT LAKE DESERT AND MOUNTAINS
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Gusty winds were observed in the northern and western parts of Utah, both due to thunderstorms and virga.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Interstate 80 sensor recorded a wind gust of 61 mph, while the Interstate 80 at mile marker 29 sensor recorded a wind gust of 64 mph. These were both due to the same passing virga.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Gusty winds were observed in the northern and western parts of Utah, both due to thunderstorms and virga.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Mud Spring RAWS recorded a 61 mph wind gust.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered thunderstorms formed over Utah on September 14th, with many of them being severe. Strong thunderstorm winds were the primary threat associated with these storms, but severe hail and flash flooding were also observed.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A semitrailer was blown over at mile marker 85 on I-80.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong and cold Pacific storm system dropped down into the Great Basin and then tracked through the Rocky Mountain region, pushing a strong cold front that spread snowfall from northwest to southeast across the area. This was an early season storm, and was the first significant winter storm for many locations, including some of the lower valleys of eastern Utah. Gusty winds also accompanied the cold front, generating areas of blowing snow, and in turn, hazardous travel conditions.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall totals ranged from 12 to 24 inches above the 8,000 foot level, with 3 to 6 inches below. Locally higher amounts were reported, including 34 inches at the Hickerson Park SNOTEL site. Winds gusted up to 40 mph, causing blowing and drifting snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong and cold Pacific storm system dropped down into the Great Basin and then tracked through the Rocky Mountain region, pushing a strong cold front that spread snowfall from northwest to southeast across the area. This was an early season storm, and was the first significant winter storm for many locations, including some of the lower valleys of eastern Utah. Gusty winds also accompanied the cold front, generating areas of blowing snow, and in turn, hazardous travel conditions.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall of 1 to 3 inches occurred across the area with locally higher amounts, including 7 inches at Blanding.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered thunderstorms formed over Utah on September 14th, with many of them being severe. Strong thunderstorm winds were the primary threat associated with these storms, but severe hail and flash flooding were also observed.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A semitrailer was blown over at mile marker 72 on I-80.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered thunderstorms formed over Utah on September 14th, with many of them being severe. Strong thunderstorm winds were the primary threat associated with these storms, but severe hail and flash flooding were also observed.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Camel Back Mountain sensor in the Dugway Proving Ground mesonet recorded a maximum wind gust of 77 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The end of September brought the first cold storm of the winter season. This storm created gusty prefrontal winds in western Utah, and dropped the first significant snowfall on the mountains of northern Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: In the first storm of the season, Chalk Creek #1 SNOTEL received 1.40 inches liquid equivalent precipitation, or approximately 16 inches of new snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong and cold Pacific storm system dropped down into the Great Basin and then tracked through the Rocky Mountain region, pushing a strong cold front that spread snowfall from northwest to southeast across the area. This was an early season storm, and was the first significant winter storm for many locations, including some of the lower valleys of eastern Utah. Gusty winds also accompanied the cold front, generating areas of blowing snow, and in turn, hazardous travel conditions.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall of 10 to 17 inches occurred across the area, with wind gusts up to 35 mph causing blowing and drifting snow.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall amounts across the area ranged from 2 to 4 inches. Wind gusts to 30 mph caused localized blowing and drifting snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The abnormal monsoon season allowed drought conditions to persist in most of southeast Utah during the month, with an escalation to moderate drought in extreme southeast Utah.  However, by the end of the month abnormally dry conditions retreated a little from the northern part of southeast Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Abnormally dry conditions persisted across the area throughout the month.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Cold air filtered into the region behind a cold front. Clearing skies and light winds overnight allowed temperatures to drop below freezing, bringing the first widespread freeze of the season to most southeastern Utah valleys.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Minimum temperatures across the area dropped to between 25 and 32 degrees.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Abnormally dry conditions persisted across most of southern Utah throughout the month, with moderate drought conditions were experienced across extreme southeastern portions of the state. A small area of moderate drought in far southeastern Utah worsened to Severe drought by the end of the month.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Moderate drought conditions that persisted across the southern portions of the area then spread into the northern sections by the end of the month.  Additionally, a small area of severe drought conditions spread into the extreme southern portion of the zone by the end of the month.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Abnormally dry conditions developed across the area by the end of the month.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold Pacific storm system and the associated cold front moved through the state of Utah, bringing heavy snow to some of the higher terrain and strong winds to the deserts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A storm total of 40 inches of snow was reported near Brian Head.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Pine Creek SNOTEL received 2.20 inches liquid equivalent precipitation for the storm, or approximately 28 inches of snow based on ratios at similar locations.

CZ_NAME: GREAT SALT LAKE DESERT AND MOUNTAINS
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold Pacific storm system and the associated cold front moved through the state of Utah, bringing heavy snow to some of the higher terrain and strong winds to the deserts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Measured gusts included 62 mph at the Wendover Point of Entry sensor, as well as 58 and 64 mph respectively at the Horizonal Grid and Callao Gate sensors in the Dugway Proving Ground mesonet.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Chepeta SNOTEL received 1.50 inches of liquid equivalent precipitation, or approximately 20 inches of snow for the event.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold Pacific storm system and the associated cold front moved through the state of Utah, bringing heavy snow to some of the higher terrain and strong winds to the deserts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A maximum wind gust of 66 mph was recorded at the I-80 at Lake Point sensor.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An unseasonably cold and potent storm system moved into Utah beginning on August 6th. There were strong winds with this storm, both ahead of and behind the associated cold front.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Milford ASOS and Brimstone Reservoir RAWS both recorded wind gusts of 58 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Several thunderstorms across the western half of Utah created severe wind gusts during the afternoon of August 5th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Camel Back Mountain sensor in the Dugway Proving Ground mesonet recorded a maximum wind gust of 78 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An unseasonably cold and potent storm system moved into Utah beginning on August 6th. There were strong winds with this storm, both ahead of and behind the associated cold front.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Recorded wind gusts in the Salt Lake and Tooele Valleys included 71 mph at South Mountain, 63 mph at Baccus, and 59 mph at Stockton Bar. The strong pre-frontal winds blew down a large billboard in downtown Salt Lake City (near I-15 and 600 South), and knocked down several trees and power lines. In addition, a semitrailer was knocked over on I-15 at the Salt Lake/Utah County line.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another fall-like storm moved into Utah in mid August. Showers and thunderstorms with this system caused strong gusty winds across the state.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Mount Baldy sensor at Deer Valley recorded a wind gust of 59 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms in southeast Utah produced heavy rainfall that resulted in flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms produced heavy rainfall that produced flash flooding off of higher surrounding terrain into the Spanish Valley area.  Runoff water one to two feet foot deep was flowing rapidly across Highway 191 as a UHP vehicle traveling between mile markers 115 and 116 was washed off the highway.  The highway was damaged in places.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms in southeast Utah produced heavy rainfall that resulted in flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms produced heavy rainfall that produced flash flooding off of higher surrounding terrain into the Moab and Spanish Valley areas.  Water one to two feet deep flowed rapidly across Highway 191 in the Spanish Valley and closed the highway.  Five condos in the Rim Village area had several inches of muddy water seeping into the residences.  Twelve people were stranded by falsh flood waters up Millcreek Canyon near Moab and had to be rescued.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Abnormally dry conditions persisted across the area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Cold air in place across the region, combined with clear skies and light winds overnight, allowed temperatures to drop below freezing. This was the first widespread freeze of the season for the Arches and Grand Flat areas of southeast Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Minimum temperatures across the area dropped to between 29 and 32 degrees.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong and cold Pacific storm system dropped down into the Great Basin and then tracked through the Rocky Mountain region, pushing a strong cold front that spread snowfall from northwest to southeast across the area. This was an early season storm, and was the first significant winter storm for many locations, including some of the lower valleys of eastern Utah. Gusty winds also accompanied the cold front, generating areas of blowing snow, and in turn, hazardous travel conditions.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall across the area amounted to about 12 inches. Wind gusts up to 35 mph were reported, causing blowing and drifting snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck an automobile in Utah County, causing one injury as well as damage to the SUV.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Four people were riding in a late model GMC Yukon SUV south on I-15, when the vehicle was struck by lightning about halfway between Santaquin and Payson. The vehicle immediately stalled. The front passenger was touching the radio/CD player at the moment lightning struck; she became unconscious and stopped breathing. Her husband administered CPR and she was resuscitated, and has since made a full recovery. The vehicle was damaged, with the electrical system and other components having to be replaced.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered thunderstorms formed over Utah on September 14th, with many of them being severe. Strong thunderstorm winds were the primary threat associated with these storms, but severe hail and flash flooding were also observed.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Fremont Junction sensor recorded a wind gust of 60 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Gusty winds were observed in the northern and western parts of Utah, both due to thunderstorms and virga.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Logan ASOS recorded a wind gust of 63 mph.
